mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A month-over-month decrease in FEDFUNDS signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% as of April 2023 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of more than 100,000 persons triggers positive 5-day forward returns as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,010 thousand as of April 2023
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflect consumer spending, the primary driver of corporate revenue and earnings. When RSAFS declines month-over-month by more than 1%, it signals demand weakness that compresses equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 486,553 million dollars as of April 2023
regime_hint: demand slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while inflation remains elevated, it signals stagflation risk that depresses equity valuations over 60 trading days as margin compression becomes evident.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 4%
regime_hint: stagflation or cost-push inflation with slowing production
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, compressing corporate earnings and equity valuations. When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, it triggers negative 20-day forward returns as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer demand strength. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously month-over-month, it signals broad-based demand destruction that produces negative 60-day forward returns as the economy enters contraction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,127 thousand units as of March 2023
fact:RSAFS at 486,553 million dollars as of April 2023
regime_hint: demand-led recession or sharp cyclical downturn
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 3 percentage points, real rates are deeply negative, supporting equity valuations through reduced discount rates and positive 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth - FEDFUNDS > 3 percentage points
regime_hint: negative real rates with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic activity. When GDPC1 annualized growth decelerates below 1% quarter-over-quarter, it signals recession risk that produces negative 60-day forward returns as equity risk premiums expand and valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,156.95 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2023
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
